code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test19-OCT-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest significant renal impairment, possibly with tubular dysfunction, and possible underlying metabolic disturbance. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.
